ProjectDescription: 500 surveys were taken within the State Park boundaries and in some of the area around it.  Sampling plan did not include a formal sample allocation or GRADSECT.  Samples were plot-based, and used occular estimation.  These were classified and the classification used in helping to label the mapped polygons.  The polygons were originally delineated on printed 1:24:000 scale photos, which were then translated into GIS format. Original coordinates were in NAD27, and then translated to NAD83 for induction to this db.
Type of protocols: Releve using occular estimation.  Plots were triangular with 50 m - 70 m legs forming an isoceles triangles.  Shape altered if vegetation (like riparian) warranted to allow the whole plot to fit homogenous vegetation. CNPS community form Rev. 95/05/19
DataContactOrganization: California Department of Fish and Wildlife - VegCAMP
DataContactRole: One of the principal investigators on the project
DataContactOrganization2: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
DataContactRole2: Primary mapper and one of the principal investigators on the project
ClassificationTool: COMPCLUS to a small degree and TWINSPAN  was more heavily relied upon
ClassificationDescription: COMPCLUS was used to see the similarity of plots using a predetermined number of seed plots.  TWINSPAN was run multiple times for the final classification.  One where species values were all relativized, another where species were set to presence/absence, reduced to just a few cover classes, also tried removing and including rare species. The final was based on the following settings 12 divisions, pseudo-species cut values set to match cover class intervals in the field forms, and no weighting on any class.
ClassificationContents: Only the data collected for the project were used in the analysis
Additional_IDdesc: PolygonID which is based on PhotoID
PlotOther1desc: Location Method: entered coordinates ( NAD27), AnzaGPS, digitized based on polys and SiteLocation
PlotOther2desc: record source: CVIS or CVIS Classic for records entered into an old foxpro application
PlotOther3desc: notes on where points were digitized
PlotOther5desc: JurisdictionID from CVIS (this is FIPS county code)
PlantStrataDescription: Pseudo-species were recorded, with separate entry for each sp in the G < 0.5, S (0.5-3 m), T > 3 m.  cover was estimated in percents AND cover classes, but were recorded in CVIS using the percent estimates.  Therefore knowledge of classes are not needed for interpretation of the dataset, so refer to documentation if interested.  The newer CVIS had HML for species strata whichwere translated back to G, S, T in this dataset.
StrataDescription: Strata were G, S, T same as above plus Total and Non-native. cover was estimated in percents AND cover classes, but were recorded in CVIS using the percent estimates.  Therefore knowledge of classes are not needed for interpretation of the dataset, so refer to documentation if interested.  The CVIS database stored the G,S,T values in fields called TallCover, MedCover, LowCover (which makes more sense, really). These fields are in AltStrata
